mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=152186.0 as of t-1mo, near cycle highs
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive PAYEMS momentum periods do not show statistically higher mean returns than negative momentum periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls below 1.75% while remaining stable for two consecutive months, the market interprets this as a sustained accommodative policy stance that lowers the equity discount rate, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=1.55 as of t-1mo, below 1.75% threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following triggered low-rate periods are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that is not captured by either series alone, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both are rising together.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=529766.0 and HOUST=1608.0 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST uptrend signal produces forward returns statistically indistinguishable from either single-series signal or from baseline (ANOVA p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC does not exceed max of individual series IC by more than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, decelerating industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals approaching capacity constraints or demand softening that historically precedes equity market drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter specifically during late-cycle conditions.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months while UNRATE is below 4%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Decelerating INDPRO during low-UNRATE regimes does not precede negative 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances, or Rank IC is not negative and significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers rapid risk-off equity selling, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.6, historically stable at cycle lows
regime_hint: any regime, most acute in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following UNRATE spikes above threshold are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or such triggers occur too infrequently (<5 instances) to support statistical inference across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Stable low CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) with month-over-month change near zero signals absence of inflationary pressure, allowing the market to price in continued accommodative policy and reduced equity risk premium, supporting marginal positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=258.82 as of t-1mo, consistent with low and stable inflation
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant positive association with low CPI volatility periods (Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of above-trend real GDP growth (GDPC1), sustained aggregate demand strength supports corporate revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, but this relationship weakens or reverses once GDP growth decelerates below trend, indicating regime dependence.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=19219.767 (billions, 2017 chained dollars) as of t-4mo, consistent with continued expansion
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during above-trend GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than during below-trend quarters (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC flips sign inconsistently across expansion sub-periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains accommodative (below 2%) while the unemployment rate simultaneously sits at multi-decade lows (below 4%), the combination signals a policy-supported tight-labor-market equilibrium in which the market prices low recession risk, jointly producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=1.55 and UNRATE=3.6 jointly satisfy trigger as of snapshot
regime_hint: accommodative policy with tight labor market
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE trigger periods do not show forward 20-day returns significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or interaction Rank IC does not exceed the maximum single-series IC by more than 0.02.
